pulse: The technology sector is currently trading at a median EV/EBITDA of 16.3x and a P/E of 23.8x, reflecting a premium relative to the broader market. Recent headlines highlight Nvidia (NVDA) posting strong revenue growth of 73.2%, yet despite this impressive performance, the stock has faced volatility as the AI rally appears to be stalling, leading analysts to suggest some semiconductor stocks may look cheap in comparison; for example, TSMC's (TSM) revenue jumped 37% in January, further underscoring ongoing AI spending. As investors navigate this landscape marked by both caution and opportunity, the divergence in performance among tech stocks indicates a critical focus on revenue growth and profitability, suggesting that companies with robust fundamentals may outperform as the market recalibrates.
